# Terraform MongoDB Atlas Project Module

 [![Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/github/release/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-project.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-project/releases/latest) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-project.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-project/commits)


Terraform module for managing MongoDB Atlas projects with full lifecycle support.
Handles project creation, backup compliance policies, maintenance windows, IP access
lists, configurable alert rules, and customer-managed encryption at rest for AWS KMS,
GCP Cloud KMS, and Azure Key Vault — all wired through the MongoDB Atlas cloud-provider
access (federated authentication) pattern to avoid IAM credential embedding.


---

This project is part of our comprehensive approach towards DevOps Acceleration.

It's 100% Open Source and licensed under the [APACHE2](LICENSE).







We have [*lots of terraform modules*][terraform_modules] that are Open Source and we are trying to get them well-maintained!. Check them out!






## Introduction

This module provisions and manages a [MongoDB Atlas](https://www.mongodb.com/atlas) project
and its associated configuration resources using the
[mongodb/mongodbatlas](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/mongodb/mongodbatlas/latest)
Terraform provider.

### What it manages

| Resource | Description |
|---|---|
| `mongodbatlas_project` | Core project with feature flags and tagging |
| `mongodbatlas_backup_compliance_policy` | Immutable backup retention rules (hourly → yearly + on-demand) |
| `mongodbatlas_maintenance_window` | Scheduled maintenance day/hour with optional auto-defer |
| `mongodbatlas_project_ip_access_list` | IP, CIDR, or AWS security-group access entries |
| `mongodbatlas_alert_configuration` | Flexible alert rules with multi-channel notifications |
| `mongodbatlas_cloud_provider_access_setup` | Cloud-provider federation (AWS IAM role, GCP SA, Azure SP) |
| `mongodbatlas_cloud_provider_access_authorization` | Authorizes the federated identity |
| `mongodbatlas_encryption_at_rest` | Customer-managed key encryption (AWS KMS / GCP KMS / Azure Key Vault) |

### Project naming

The Atlas project name is resolved in this order:

1. `name` variable — used verbatim when set.
2. `name_prefix` + auto-generated suffix — `<name_prefix>-<org_unit>-<env_name>-<env_type>`.

### Encryption at rest

Encryption at rest is enabled through two separate variables to avoid Terraform dependency
cycles between the Atlas module and the cloud-provider module that provisions the KMS key:

- `encryption_provider` — plain string (`"AWS"`, `"GCP"`, or `"AZURE"`).
  This controls whether `mongodbatlas_cloud_provider_access_setup` is created.
  The cloud module can read the resulting outputs (role ARN, GCP SA email, Azure app ID)
  to build its own resources, then pass their attributes back via `encryption_provider_config`.
- `encryption_provider_config` — cloud-specific key identifiers and (for AWS) the IAM role ARN.

### Alert import helper

Setting `generate_import = true` produces two outputs:

- `imported_alert_statement` — ready-to-run `tofu import` commands for every existing alert.
- `imported_alert_json` — JSON representation of current alerts suitable for copying into
  the `settings.alerts` list.

## Usage


**IMPORTANT:** The `master` branch is used in `source` just as an example. In your code, do not pin to `master` because there may be breaking changes between releases.
Instead pin to the release tag (e.g. `?ref=vX.Y.Z`) of one of our [latest releases](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-project/releases).


```hcl
# terragrunt.hcl (deployment-level override)
terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-project.git//?ref=v1.0.0"
}

inputs = {
  # ── Project naming ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  name_prefix: ""         # (Optional) Prefix prepended to auto-generated name. Default: "".
  # name: ""              # (Optional) Explicit project name; overrides name_prefix. Default: "".

  # ── Atlas organisation ─────────────────────────────────────────
  organization_id: ""     # (Optional) Direct MongoDB Atlas org ID. Default: "".
  # organization_name: "" # (Optional) Atlas org name resolved via data source. Default: "".

  # ── Module settings ────────────────────────────────────────────
  settings: {
    # Project-level feature flags
    default_alerts_settings: true                       # (Optional) Keep Atlas default alert settings. Default: null.
    collect_database_specifics_statistics_enabled: true # (Optional) Collect DB-specific statistics. Default: null.
    data_explorer_enabled: true                         # (Optional) Enable Data Explorer UI. Default: null.
    extended_storage_sizes_enabled: false               # (Optional) Allow extended storage tiers. Default: null.
    performance_advisor_enabled: true                   # (Optional) Enable Performance Advisor. Default: null.
    schema_advisor_enabled: true                        # (Optional) Enable Schema Advisor. Default: null.

    # Backup compliance policy
    # WARNING: Once enabled this policy cannot be disabled without Atlas support.
    backup_compliance: {
      enabled: false                    # (Optional) Enforce backup compliance. Default: false.
      authorized_user: {
        email: ""                       # (Required when enabled) Authorised user email.
        first_name: ""                  # (Required when enabled) Authorised user first name.
        last_name: ""                   # (Required when enabled) Authorised user last name.
      }
      copy_protection_enabled: false    # (Optional) Prevent backup deletion. Default: false.
      pit_enabled: false                # (Optional) Enable Point-in-Time recovery. Default: false.
      encryption_at_rest_enabled: false # (Optional) Require backups to be encrypted. Default: false.
      restore_window_days: 7            # (Optional) Restore window in days. Default: 7.
      hourly: {
        interval: 6                     # (Optional) Snapshot every N hours (1,2,4,6,8,12). Default: 1.
        retention_unit: "days"          # (Optional) Retention unit. Values: "days". Default: "days".
        retention_value: 2              # (Optional) Number of units to retain. Default: 1.
      }
      daily: {
        interval: 1                     # (Optional) Must be 1. Default: 1.
        retention_unit: "days"          # (Optional) Retention unit. Values: "days". Default: "days".
        retention_value: 7              # (Optional) Number of units to retain. Default: 7.
      }
      weekly: {
        interval: 6                     # (Optional) Day of week (1=Sunday…7=Saturday). Default: 1.
        retention_unit: "weeks"         # (Optional) Retention unit. Values: "weeks". Default: "weeks".
        retention_value: 4              # (Optional) Number of units to retain. Default: 4.
      }
      monthly: {
        interval: 28                    # (Optional) Day of month (1–28). Default: 1.
        retention_unit: "months"        # (Optional) Retention unit. Values: "months". Default: "months".
        retention_value: 12             # (Optional) Number of units to retain. Default: 12.
      }
      yearly: {
        interval: 1                     # (Optional) Month of year (1–12). Default: 1.
        retention_unit: "years"         # (Optional) Retention unit. Values: "years". Default: "years".
        retention_value: 2              # (Optional) Number of units to retain. Default: 2.
      }
      on_demand: {
        interval: 0                     # (Optional) Must be 0. Default: 1.
        retention_unit: "days"          # (Optional) Retention unit. Values: "days". Default: "days".
        retention_value: 7              # (Optional) Number of units to retain. Default: 7.
      }
    }

    # Maintenance window
    maintenance: {
      enabled: false                    # (Optional) Enable scheduled maintenance window. Default: false.
      day_of_week: 1                    # (Optional) Day of week (1=Sunday…7=Saturday). Default: 1.
      hour_of_day: 0                    # (Optional) Hour in UTC (0–23). Default: 0.
      defer: null                       # (Optional) Defer the next occurrence. Default: null.
      auto_defer: null                  # (Optional) Automatically defer maintenance. Default: null.
      auto_defer_once_enabled: null     # (Optional) Auto-defer once. Default: null.
    }

    # IP access list — map keyed by a unique entry ID.
    # Each entry uses exactly one of: ip_address, cidr_block, aws_security_group.
    access_list: {}
    # access_list:
    #   office:
    #     cidr_block: "203.0.113.0/24"
    #     comment: "Office network"
    #   bastion:
    #     ip_address: "198.51.100.5"
    #     comment: "Bastion host"
    #   app_sg:
    #     aws_security_group: "sg-0abc123def456"
    #     comment: "Application security group"

    # Encryption at rest — set enabled: true and pair with encryption_provider
    # and encryption_provider_config to activate CMK encryption.
    encryption_at_rest: {
      enabled: false                    # (Optional) Enable Customer-Managed Key encryption. Default: false.
    }

    # Alert event types excluded from this module's for_each loop.
    # Use this when a cloud-specific companion module manages certain alert types
    # to prevent resource conflicts.
    excluded_alert_event_types: []     # (Optional) List of event_type strings to skip. Default: [].

    # Alert configurations — list of alert rules.
    alerts: []
    # alerts:
    #   - event_type: "REPLICATION_OPLOG_WINDOW_RUNNING_OUT"  # (Required) Atlas event type.
    #     enabled: true                                        # (Optional) Default: true.
    #     notifications:
    #       - type_name: "GROUP"                              # (Required) Values: EMAIL, SMS, GROUP, ORG,
    #                                                         #   TEAM, SLACK, DATADOG, OPS_GENIE,
    #                                                         #   VICTOR_OPS, WEBHOOK, MICROSOFT_TEAMS, PAGER_DUTY.
    #         roles: ["GROUP_OWNER"]
    #         interval_min: 60
    #         delay_min: 0
    #         email_enabled: true
    #     threshold_config:
    #       operator: "LESS_THAN"                            # (Optional) Values: GREATER_THAN, LESS_THAN.
    #       threshold: 1
    #       units: "HOURS"
  }

  # ── Encryption provider ────────────────────────────────────────
  # Plain string — must NOT reference cloud resource attributes to avoid dependency cycles.
  encryption_provider: ""   # (Optional) Cloud provider for CMK encryption. Values: "AWS", "GCP", "AZURE". Default: "".

  # Cloud-specific key configuration — populated by the cloud companion module
  # after cloud_provider_setup outputs are available.
  # (Sensitive — contains key ARNs / identifiers.)
  encryption_provider_config: {}
  # AWS:
  #   iam_assumed_role_arn: ""          # (Required for AWS) IAM role ARN Atlas will assume.
  #   customer_master_key_id: ""        # (Required for AWS) KMS CMK ARN.
  #   region: "US_EAST_1"              # (Required for AWS) Atlas-format region (UPPER_UNDERSCORE).
  # GCP:
  #   key_version_resource_id: ""       # (Required for GCP) Full KMS crypto key version resource name.
  # AZURE:
  #   azure_environment: "AZURE"        # (Optional) Values: AZURE, AZURE_CHINA, AZURE_GERMANY, AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT.
  #   subscription_id: ""              # (Required for AZURE) Azure subscription ID.
  #   resource_group_name: ""          # (Required for AZURE) Resource group containing the Key Vault.
  #   key_vault_name: ""               # (Required for AZURE) Key Vault name.
  #   key_identifier: ""               # (Required for AZURE) Versioned Key Vault key URI.

  # ── Import helper ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  # generate_import: false  # (Optional) Output tofu import commands for existing alerts. Default: false.
}
```

## Quick Start

1. **Initialise** the module using the MongoDB Atlas boilerplate:
   ```sh
   make init/mongodb
   ```

2. **Configure** the deployment by editing `.boilerplate/inputs.yaml`.
   At minimum set `organization_id` (or `organization_name`).

3. **Format and lint** before committing:
   ```sh
   make fmt
   make lint
   ```

4. **Apply** via Terragrunt from the deployment directory:
   ```sh
   terragrunt apply
   ```

5. **(Optional) Import existing alerts** — set `generate_import: true`, apply once,
   then copy the `imported_alert_statement` output into your shell:
   ```sh
   terragrunt output -raw imported_alert_statement | bash
   ```
   Copy `imported_alert_json` into `settings.alerts` in `inputs.yaml`, then set
   `generate_import: false` and apply again.

### Outputs reference

| Output | Description |
|---|---|
| `project_name` | Resolved Atlas project name |
| `project_id` | MongoDB Atlas project ID |
| `project_creation_timestamp` | ISO-8601 creation timestamp |
| `project_backup_policy_id` | Backup compliance policy ID (null when disabled) |
| `cloud_provider_setup_role_id` | Atlas role ID from cloud provider access setup |
| `cloud_provider_setup_aws_account_arn` | Atlas AWS account ARN for IAM trust policy (AWS) |
| `cloud_provider_setup_aws_external_id` | External ID for STS AssumeRole condition (AWS) |
| `cloud_provider_setup_gcp_service_account` | Atlas GCP SA email — grant `roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyEncrypterDecrypter` (GCP) |
| `cloud_provider_setup_azure_app_id` | Atlas Azure app ID / `client_id` (AZURE) |
| `cloud_provider_setup_azure_service_principal_id` | Atlas Azure SP object ID — grant Key Vault access (AZURE) |
| `cloud_provider_setup_azure_tenant_id` | Azure AD tenant ID (AZURE) |
| `encryption_at_rest_id` | Encryption-at-rest configuration ID |
| `all_alert_configurations` | Raw alert configurations (used by cloud companion modules) |
| `imported_alert_statement` | `tofu import` commands for existing alerts (when `generate_import = true`) |
| `imported_alert_json` | JSON of current alerts for copying into `settings.alerts` (when `generate_import = true`) |


## Examples

### Minimal project — org ID + default alerts

```hcl
# inputs.yaml
organization_id: "5f1234abcd5678ef12345678"

settings:
  default_alerts_settings: true
  performance_advisor_enabled: true
  schema_advisor_enabled: true
```

### Project with backup compliance and maintenance window

```hcl
# inputs.yaml
organization_id: "5f1234abcd5678ef12345678"

settings:
  backup_compliance:
    enabled: true
    authorized_user:
      email: "admin@example.com"
      first_name: "Jane"
      last_name: "Doe"
    pit_enabled: true
    restore_window_days: 7
    daily:
      interval: 1
      retention_unit: "days"
      retention_value: 14
    weekly:
      interval: 7
      retention_unit: "weeks"
      retention_value: 8

  maintenance:
    enabled: true
    day_of_week: 7    # Saturday
    hour_of_day: 3    # 03:00 UTC
```

### Project with AWS KMS encryption at rest

```hcl
# inputs.yaml (Atlas module)
organization_id: "5f1234abcd5678ef12345678"
encryption_provider: "AWS"

settings:
  encryption_at_rest:
    enabled: true

# encryption_provider_config is injected by the cloud companion module
# after it reads cloud_provider_setup_aws_account_arn and
# cloud_provider_setup_aws_external_id from this module's outputs.
```

### Alert with Slack notification

```hcl
# inputs.yaml
settings:
  alerts:
    - event_type: "REPLICATION_OPLOG_WINDOW_RUNNING_OUT"
      enabled: true
      notifications:
        - type_name: "SLACK"
          api_token: "xoxb-..."
          channel_name: "#atlas-alerts"
          interval_min: 30
          delay_min: 0
      threshold_config:
        operator: "LESS_THAN"
        threshold: 1
        units: "HOURS"
```

### IP access list with mixed entry types

```hcl
# inputs.yaml
settings:
  access_list:
    office:
      cidr_block: "203.0.113.0/24"
      comment: "Corporate office CIDR"
    bastion:
      ip_address: "198.51.100.5"
      comment: "Bastion host"
    app_tier:
      aws_security_group: "sg-0abc123def456"
      comment: "Application security group"
```



## Makefile Targets
```
Available targets:

  help                                Help screen
  help/all                            Display help for all targets
  help/short                          This help short screen
  init/%                              Initialize the project for a specific cloud provider: %S
  lint                                Lint terraform/opentofu code
  tag                                 Tag the current version

```

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|:--------:|
| <a name="input_encryption_provider"></a> [encryption\_provider](#input\_encryption\_provider) | (Optional) Cloud provider for encryption at rest. Values: AWS, GCP, AZURE. Passed separately from encryption\_provider\_config to avoid Terraform dependency cycles. | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_encryption_provider_config"></a> [encryption\_provider\_config](#input\_encryption\_provider\_config) | (Optional) Cloud-provider-specific encryption configuration passed from the cloud module. AWS: IAM role ARN + KMS key ARN + region. GCP: KMS key version resource ID. AZURE: subscription ID + resource group + key vault name + key identifier. Auth identifiers (GCP SA email, Azure app/SP/tenant) are sourced from mongodbatlas\_cloud\_provider\_access\_setup outputs. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_extra_tags"></a> [extra\_tags](#input\_extra\_tags) | Extra tags to add to the resources | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_generate_import"></a> [generate\_import](#input\_generate\_import) | (Optional) Generate OpenTofu import blocks for existing Atlas resources | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_is_hub"></a> [is\_hub](#input\_is\_hub) | Is this a hub or spoke configuration? | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_name"></a> [name](#input\_name) | (Optional) Explicit name for the MongoDB Atlas project; overrides name\_prefix | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_name_prefix"></a> [name\_prefix](#input\_name\_prefix) | (Optional) Prefix for the name of the resources | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_org"></a> [org](#input\_org) | Organization details | <pre>object({<br/>    organization_name = string<br/>    organization_unit = string<br/>    environment_type  = string<br/>    environment_name  = string<br/>  })</pre> | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_organization_id"></a> [organization\_id](#input\_organization\_id) | (Optional) The ID of the MongoDB Atlas organization where the project will be created | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_organization_name"></a> [organization\_name](#input\_organization\_name) | (Optional) The name of the MongoDB Atlas organization where the project will be created | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_settings"></a> [settings](#input\_settings) | (Optional) Module settings object controlling project features: backup compliance, maintenance window, IP access list, and alert configurations | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_spoke_def"></a> [spoke\_def](#input\_spoke\_def) | Spoke ID Number, must be a 3 digit number | `string` | `"001"` | no |

## Outputs

| Name | Description |
|------|-------------|
| <a name="output_all_alert_configurations"></a> [all\_alert\_configurations](#output\_all\_alert\_configurations) | Raw alert configurations fetched from the project (used by cloud-specific modules for provider-specific alert import) |
| <a name="output_cloud_provider_setup_aws_account_arn"></a> [cloud\_provider\_setup\_aws\_account\_arn](#output\_cloud\_provider\_setup\_aws\_account\_arn) | Atlas AWS account ARN used for the IAM trust relationship (AWS) |
| <a name="output_cloud_provider_setup_aws_external_id"></a> [cloud\_provider\_setup\_aws\_external\_id](#output\_cloud\_provider\_setup\_aws\_external\_id) | Atlas external ID for the IAM STS AssumeRole condition (AWS) |
| <a name="output_cloud_provider_setup_azure_app_id"></a> [cloud\_provider\_setup\_azure\_app\_id](#output\_cloud\_provider\_setup\_azure\_app\_id) | Atlas Azure application ID — used as client\_id in the Key Vault encryption config (AZURE) |
| <a name="output_cloud_provider_setup_azure_service_principal_id"></a> [cloud\_provider\_setup\_azure\_service\_principal\_id](#output\_cloud\_provider\_setup\_azure\_service\_principal\_id) | Atlas Azure service principal object ID — grant this SP access to the Key Vault (AZURE) |
| <a name="output_cloud_provider_setup_azure_tenant_id"></a> [cloud\_provider\_setup\_azure\_tenant\_id](#output\_cloud\_provider\_setup\_azure\_tenant\_id) | Azure AD tenant ID associated with the Atlas service principal (AZURE) |
| <a name="output_cloud_provider_setup_gcp_service_account"></a> [cloud\_provider\_setup\_gcp\_service\_account](#output\_cloud\_provider\_setup\_gcp\_service\_account) | Atlas GCP service account email — grant this SA roles/cloudkms.cryptoKeyEncrypterDecrypter on the KMS key (GCP) |
| <a name="output_cloud_provider_setup_role_id"></a> [cloud\_provider\_setup\_role\_id](#output\_cloud\_provider\_setup\_role\_id) | MongoDB Atlas role ID from the cloud provider access setup |
| <a name="output_encryption_at_rest_id"></a> [encryption\_at\_rest\_id](#output\_encryption\_at\_rest\_id) | MongoDB Atlas encryption-at-rest configuration ID |
| <a name="output_imported_alert_json"></a> [imported\_alert\_json](#output\_imported\_alert\_json) | JSON representation of current Atlas alert configurations suitable for copying into settings.alerts (only populated when generate\_import = true) |
| <a name="output_imported_alert_statement"></a> [imported\_alert\_statement](#output\_imported\_alert\_statement) | Ready-to-run `tofu import` commands for every existing alert in the project (only populated when generate\_import = true) |
| <a name="output_project_backup_policy_id"></a> [project\_backup\_policy\_id](#output\_project\_backup\_policy\_id) | Backup compliance policy ID; null when backup compliance is disabled |
| <a name="output_project_creation_timestamp"></a> [project\_creation\_timestamp](#output\_project\_creation\_timestamp) | ISO-8601 timestamp of when the Atlas project was created |
| <a name="output_project_id"></a> [project\_id](#output\_project\_id) | MongoDB Atlas project ID |
| <a name="output_project_name"></a> [project\_name](#output\_project\_name) | Resolved MongoDB Atlas project name |
